analysis: Seven nights, and the trajectory on this one is genuinely hot — you went from scraping 6h 13m on 28 June to landing three nights in a row above 7h 30m to close out the stretch, and that last night clocked 2h 34m of REM which is the best number in the whole window. Awake time also collapsed beautifully by the end, down to 19 minutes on the third of July, which means whatever combination of the cold blast and the lavender and the yin yoga is hitting, it is actually hitting. The gap worth noticing is deep sleep, which started strong around 45–51 minutes in the earlier nights and has quietly drifted down to the low 30s as total sleep improved — nothing catastrophic, just a thing to watch. Overall though, the second half of this stretch looks like a man who figured something out.
